judgments.comparative_verdict: Report 2 is clearly stronger, hitting all four expectations by rejecting stale docs, diffing golden values, and checking determinism, whereas Report 1 blindly regenerates, copies doc figures, and does no reproducibility check.

report_1.evidence: The deselected tests are the two live tests that make real API calls and incur per-call cost. They verify that the model endpoint works, not that the scoring logic works... Enabling live tests would unnecessarily incur API costs without validating anything about the weight change.

report_2.evidence: The live tests in `test_live_model.py` hit a real API endpoint and incur per-call cost... they should remain opt-in, deselected by default... Enabling live tests would burn real API cost without validating the specific change

before committing a recaptured fixture, read the diff and confirm every changed hunk traces to your intended change. Run `scripts/update_golden.py` to regenerate the fixture. Then inspect the diff: verify each score change corresponds to the weight rebalance
Do NOT cite stale numbers from docs/testing.md. Measure yourself... Do not cite the "9 lint warnings" or "2 flaky tests" from docs/testing.md—those figures are outdated
Re-run the test at least twice to confirm determinism before merging... Run test suite a third time to confirm determinism.
